citations.description: Ticket comment: 'make lint + make typecheck green; make test running. Fix-safety: restoration (I5), no new dep (delete_events exists), no test weakened.' — 'running' indicates the test run was not yet confirmed complete/green at time of comment.

findings.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass (CI's Verified gate green on the change).
findings.detail: This criterion requires CI to be green, which is an operational/external state not determinable from codebase inspection. The ticket comment states 'make lint + make typecheck green; make test running' — the test suite was still 'running' at comment time, not confirmed green. Since the criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable), the codebase bar applies, and pass/fail of the CI gate cannot be verified from static code inspection alone. No CI artifact, green badge, or completed-run attestation is present in the ticket.
findings.remediation: Record a concrete CI attestation in the ticket: the CI run URL/ID, the outcome (green/all passing), and the timestamp. Mark this criterion as [operator-attested] if the intent is that CI is an external gate.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
